sections.title: SKILLS
bullets: Languages: Python, Java, C/C++, Kotlin, HTML, SQL
Frameworks & Libraries: OpenCV, MediaPipe, Numpy, Android SDK, Room Persistence Library
Tools & Platforms: Git, Linux, Google Play Console, Android Studio
Concepts: Algorithms & Data Structures, Object-Oriented Design, Computer Vision, Full-Stack Development

entries.title: Undergraduate Research Assistant
bullets: Took charge of the development of two full-stack applications including HandEase and Palm & Plant to assist in Parkinson's rehabilitation, projecting to aid medically patients in hospitals across 10+ countries.

Developed a computer vision pipeline using Python, OpenCV, and MediaPipe – AI hand library to detect and collect rehabilitation data on 7 hand exercise gestures of patients, resulting in ~90% accuracy for medical analysis.
Optimized Apps to run in real time (<100ms latency) on local hardware, delivering immediate results and feedback to create polished and consistent user experiences.
Designed dashboards with activity heatmap and daily metrics using Python to improve monitoring efficiency for practitioners by 30% and streamline long-term medical care for patients.
Led end-to-end project lifecycle from requirements, development, testing, and deployment, which was presented in the 11th Singapore International Parkinson Disease & Movement Disorders Symposium.
Engineered 10 applications with 4 team members and updated weekly progress to clinicians at the Djavad Mowafaghian Centre for Brain Health, receiving positive reviews.
entries.subtitle: University of British Columbia

entries.title: Math & Reading Instructor
bullets: Invited by Kumon Supervisor to join their team due to outstanding performance in Math and Literature
Mentored 100+ students in math/reading, marked their homework, adapted lessons to individual needs, and provided optimal and precise guidance
Managed class flow and delivered feedback according to students personal understanding and performance, leading to students’ significant improvement in tests and classroom behavior
entries.subtitle: Kumon Inc.

entries.title: My Fridge App
bullets: Designed an Android application managing fridge inventory and published on Google Play, reducing food waste by 20% and inducing healthy eating habits.
Implemented Room DB + push notifications reminding users of expiry dates and track shopping lists with >95% reliability, constructing efficient food usage and enhancing shopping experiences.

entries.title: RoboCup Junior Maze Rescue - Captain
bullets: Architected a prioritized maze navigation algorithm with modified Dijkstra’s and LiDAR-based positioning for multi-level point-based maps.
Facilitated CV logic for letter and color detection with >90% accuracy, reacting to maze events effectively.

Designed a robot achieving 85% maze success rate and completing runs <2 mins, which led team to place 2nd in the Supergroup Category and 17th in the Individual Component out of 50+ teams at RoboCup International 2023.

entries.title: Game Development Portfolio
bullets: Created 3 Java games of ~3000 LOC to strengthen OOP/design skills, and implemented DFS group capture for Go, randomized cascade reveal for Minesweeper, and custom 2D physics in platformer parkour game.

sections.title: AWARDS AND ACHIEVEMENTS
bullets: International Collegiate Programming Contest Local Competition (Waterloo) | Rank 28 out of ~100
RoboCup International Junior Maze Rescue | 2nd Place Supergroup, 17th Individual
Avogadro’s Chemistry Contest | Certificate of Distinction
AIME (American Invitational Mathematics Examination) Qualifier (top >10% of 150,000)
COMC (Canadian Open Mathematics Challenge) Distinction
